⏺ Bash(gh pr view --json title,body,baseRefName,headRefName,number 2>/dev/null || echo "No PR found") ⎿ {"baseRefName":"master","body":"This change consolidates the fragmented checkout/implementation-setup flow and fixes a critical Graphite divergence bug where stacked PRs retrack before rebase, causing the cached SHA to become stale. The solution introduces `erk pr prepare` as the clean, idempotent way to set up impl-context after checkout.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n- **Fix Graphite retrack ordering in `pr checkout`** — Move retrack logic after rebase completes so cached SHA matches post-rebase commit, eliminating divergence for stacked PRs. Add `update_local_ref` sync for locally-stale parent branches.\n- **Extract shared impl-context creation** — New `create_impl_context_from_pr()` function in `setup_impl_from_pr.py` handles reading plan content and creating `.erk/impl-context/` folder (no checkout/worktree logic).\n- **Add `erk pr prepare` command** — New standalone command that auto-detects plan number from current branch's PR or accepts explicit number. Idempotent: skips recreation if impl-context already exists.\n- **Update error messaging** — `pr checkout` now suggests `erk pr prepare` as alternative to `erk br co --for-plan` when plan not yet implemented.\n- **Comprehensive test coverage** — Updated Graphite linking tests to verify retrack-after-rebase ordering; added tests for stacked PR rebase-then-track flow, prepare command (auto-detect, explicit number, errors, idempotency), and `create_impl_context_from_pr()` function behavior.\n\n<details>\n<summary>Files Changed</summary>\n\n### Added (2 files)\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/prepare_cmd.py` — New `erk pr prepare` command for impl-context setup\n- `tests/commands/pr/test_prepare.py` — Tests for prepare command (auto-detect, explicit number, errors, idempotency)\n\n### Modified (5 files)\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py` — Fix Graphite retrack ordering (move after rebase), add parent branch sync logic, update error message\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py` — Extract `create_impl_context_from_pr()` function; refactor `_setup_planned_pr_plan()` to call it\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py` — Import and register `pr_prepare` command\n- `tests/commands/pr/test_checkout_graphite_linking.py` — Update docstring for retrack timing; add `test_pr_checkout_stacked_pr_rebases_then_tracks`\n- `tests/commands/pr/test_checkout.py` — Update test description for base branch sync behavior\n- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_setup_impl_from_pr.py` — Add layer 4 unit tests for `create_impl_context_from_pr()` covering ref.json validation, missing fields, and error cases\n\n</details>\n\n## User Experience\n\n**Before:**\n```\n$ erk pr checkout 120 # Stacked PR\nCreated worktree...\n$ gt log # Graphite divergence warning!\n```\n\n**After:**\n```\n$ erk pr checkout 120 # Stacked PR\nCreated worktree...\nRebasing onto base branch...\nTracking branch with Graphite...\n$ gt log # No divergence, SHA is consistent\n\n$ erk pr prepare # Auto-detect from current branch\nPrepared impl-context for plan #120\n\n$ erk pr prepare 300 # Explicit plan number\nPrepared impl-context for plan #300\n```\n\n## Critical Notes\n\n- `erk exec setup-impl` and `erk exec setup-impl-from-pr` remain unchanged for CI/skill compatibility; they are not deprecated in this change.\n- The Graphite retrack behavior change only affects stacked PRs (PRs with non-trunk base branches) and already-tracked branches. New/untracked branches use `track_branch()` as before.\n\n<details>\n<summary>original-plan</summary>\n\n# Consolidate checkout/implementation-setup flow + fix Graphite divergence\n\n## Context\n\n`erk pr checkout` causes Graphite divergence for stacked PRs (retracks before rebase). Meanwhile, the checkout/implementation-setup flow is fragmented across 6+ entry points with overlapping responsibilities. The user wants to:\n\n1. Fix the Graphite divergence bug\n2. Add `erk pr prepare` as the clean way to set up impl-context for a checked-out PR\n3. Rationalize the relationship between the overlapping commands\n\n## Current Landscape\n\n| Command | Checkout? | Worktree? | Rebase stacked? | Graphite? | Impl-context? |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `erk pr checkout <N>` | Yes | Yes | Yes (buggy) | Yes (buggy) | No |\n| `erk pr checkout P<N>` | Yes | Yes | No | No | No |\n| `erk br co --for-plan <N>` | Yes | Yes | Yes (correct) | Yes (correct) | Yes |\n| `erk exec setup-impl` | Yes | No | No | No | Yes |\n| `erk exec setup-impl-from-pr` | Yes | No | No | No | Yes |\n\nThe intended clean flow after this change:\n```\nerk pr checkout <N> → get code into a worktree (fix Graphite bug here)\nerk pr prepare [<N>] → set up impl-context for current worktree's PR\n```\n\n## Changes\n\n### Step 1: Fix Graphite divergence in `erk pr checkout`\n\n**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py`\n\n**Bug:** Lines 256-262 retrack with Graphite before the stacked-PR rebase at line 283. Graphite's cached SHA becomes stale after rebase.\n\n**Fix:** Remove the early retrack block (lines 256-262). Move all Graphite tracking to after the rebase block (after line 290), consolidating the \"already tracked\" and \"untracked\" cases into one block. The flow becomes:\n\n```\nfetch + force-update → create worktree → rebase (if stacked) → retrack/track\n```\n\nFor stacked PRs where the parent branch exists locally but is stale, also add `update_local_ref` logic matching `_rebase_and_track_for_plan()` (branch/checkout_cmd.py:381-388).\n\n**Test update:** `tests/commands/pr/test_checkout_graphite_linking.py`\n- Update `test_pr_checkout_retracks_diverged_graphite_branch` (line 232) — its docstring explicitly says retrack happens \"before worktree creation or rebase\", which is the buggy behavior. Update to verify retrack happens after rebase.\n- Add a new test for stacked PR checkout that verifies rebase-then-track ordering.\n\n### Step 2: Extract shared impl-context creation logic\n\n**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py`\n\nExtract the \"read plan content and create impl folder\" portion of `_setup_planned_pr_plan()` into a standalone function:\n\n```python\ndef create_impl_context_from_pr(\n ctx: click.Context,\n *,\n plan_number: int,\n cwd: Path,\n branch_name: str,\n) -> dict[str, str | int | bool | None]:\n```\n\nThis function:\n- Fetches PR metadata, reads plan content (from committed `.erk/impl-context/` files or PR body fallback)\n- Creates `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan.md and ref.json\n- Does NOT checkout branches or create worktrees\n\nHave `_setup_planned_pr_plan()` call this after its branch checkout logic.\n\n### Step 3: Create `erk pr prepare` command\n\n**New file:** `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/prepare_cmd.py`\n\n```\nerk pr prepare [PLAN_NUMBER]\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- Must be run from inside a worktree\n- If `PLAN_NUMBER` omitted: detect from current branch's PR (via `ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch()`)\n- Calls `create_impl_context_from_pr()` from Step 2\n- Idempotent: if impl-context already exists for this plan, reports success without recreating\n\n**Register in:** `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py` — add import and `pr_group.add_command(pr_prepare, name=\"prepare\")`\n\n**New test file:** `tests/commands/pr/test_prepare.py`\n- Test auto-detection from current branch\n- Test explicit plan number\n- Test error when not on a plan branch and no number provided\n- Test idempotent behavior (impl-context already exists)\n\n### Step 4: Update references\n\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py` line 371: The `_checkout_plan` error message says `erk br co --for-plan {plan_number}` — add `erk pr prepare` as alternative\n- Do NOT deprecate `erk br co --for-plan` yet (it has extensive TUI references). That's a follow-up.\n\n## Files Modified\n\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py` — fix Graphite ordering, update help text\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py` — extract shared function\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/prepare_cmd.py` — **new** command\n- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py` — register new command\n- `tests/commands/pr/test_checkout_graphite_linking.py` — update/add tests\n- `tests/commands/pr/test_prepare.py` — **new** test file\n\n## Files NOT Modified\n\n- `erk exec setup-impl` / `erk exec setup-impl-from-pr` — must remain as-is for CI and skill compatibility\n- `erk br co --for-plan` — deprecation is a follow-up\n- `erk implement` — orthogonal command\n- `.github/workflows/plan-implement.yml` — uses exec commands, unaffected\n\n## Verification\n\n1. Run Graphite linking tests: `pytest tests/commands/pr/test_checkout_graphite_linking.py`\n2. Run prepare tests: `pytest tests/commands/pr/test_prepare.py`\n3. Run all PR checkout tests: `pytest tests/commands/pr/ -k checkout`\n4. Manual: `erk pr checkout <stacked-pr>` then `gt ls` — no divergence warning\n5. Manual: `erk pr checkout <N>` then `erk pr prepare` — creates impl-context\n\n\n</details>\n\n<!-- WARNING: Machine-generated. Manual edits may break erk tooling. -->\n<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->\n<details>\n<summary>plan-header</summary>\n\n```yaml\n\nschema_version: '2'\ncreated_at: '2026-03-06T04:52:59.501262+00:00'\ncreated_by: schrockn\nplan_comment_id: null\nlast_dispatched_run_id: '22764377822'\nlast_dispatched_node_id: WFR_kwLOPxC3hc8AAAAFTNzW3g\nlast_dispatched_at: '2026-03-06T12:56:09.838463+00:00'\nlast_local_impl_at: null\nlast_local_impl_event: null\nlast_local_impl_session: null\nlast_local_impl_user: null\nlast_remote_impl_at: '2026-03-06T13:07:22+00:00'\nlast_remote_impl_run_id: '22764377822'\nlast_remote_impl_session_id: 1a6bde5d-6d69-4af9-b042-698b6a69623c\nbranch_name: plnd/consolidate-checkout-prepa-03-06-0452\ncreated_from_session: e5cd63de-5071-4e8c-b74f-60c16338541c\nlifecycle_stage: impl\nworktree_name: erk\nlast_session_branch: async-learn/8830\nlast_session_id: 1a6bde5d-6d69-4af9-b042-698b6a69623c\nlast_session_at: '2026-03-06T13:07:18.241015+00:00'\nlast_session_source: remote\n\n```\n\n</details>\n<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->\n---\n\n\nTo replicate this PR locally, run:\n\n```\nerk pr teleport 8830\n```\n","headRefName":"plnd/consolidate-checkout-prepa-03-06-0452","number":8830,"title":"Fix Graphite divergence in pr checkout + add pr prepare command + extract shared impl-context creation"}

⏺ Here are the DRY violations I found across these code paths:
--- 1. Stacked PR rebase + parent branch sync (most significant)
The "fetch parent, update local ref, rebase onto parent" logic is duplicated nearly verbatim:
- pr/checkout_cmd.py:261-290 (_checkout_pr) - branch/checkout_cmd.py:346-400 (_rebase_and_track_for_plan)
The PR description literally says _rebase_and_track_for_plan "mirrors the pattern from pr/checkout_cmd.py for stacked PRs." Both do:
1. Check if parent branch exists locally 2. If not: fetch + create tracking branch 3. If yes: fetch, compare SHAs, update local ref if stale 4. Rebase onto origin/{parent} 5. Handle rebase conflicts with abort + warning
This should be a single shared function.
--- 2. Idempotent impl-context check
The "already set up?" guard is duplicated:
- prepare_cmd.py:69-75 - setup_impl_from_pr.py:243-261 (_setup_planned_pr_plan)
Both do: impl_dir = resolve_impl_dir(cwd, branch_name=...) if impl_dir is not None: existing_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir) if existing_ref is not None and existing_ref.plan_id == str(plan_number): # already exists, skip
--- 3. _setup_impl_for_plan not consolidated with create_impl_context_from_pr
The PR extracted create_impl_context_from_pr as the shared impl-context creator, but branch/checkout_cmd.py:290-325 (_setup_impl_for_plan) still does its own create_impl_folder() + save_plan_ref() calls independently. The --for-plan path could call create_impl_context_from_pr (or a shared lower-level function) instead of duplicating the folder creation + ref saving.
--- 4. _rebase_and_track_for_plan + _setup_impl_for_plan call sequence repeated 3x
In branch/checkout_cmd.py, the same two-call sequence appears at lines 641-656, 676-703, and 739-753:
_rebase_and_track_for_plan(ctx, ...) _setup_impl_for_plan(ctx, ...)
Each site also follows with _perform_checkout(...). This suggests a missing helper like _complete_plan_checkout(ctx, *, target_worktree, setup, ...) that bundles the rebase → impl-setup → checkout sequence.
--- Summary
The biggest win would be extracting the stacked-PR rebase logic (#1) into a shared function — it's ~30 lines of non-trivial git orchestration copied between two files. The idempotent check (#2) and the 3x call sequence (#4) are smaller but easy consolidations. And #3 represents the "expected code removal" you noticed was missing — _setup_impl_for_plan should delegate to the newly extracted create_impl_context_from_pr rather than doing its own impl folder creation.

--- Consolidate DRY violations in checkout/impl-context code paths
Context: PR #8830 extracted create_impl_context_from_pr() and added erk pr prepare, but left significant code duplication. This addresses 4 DRY violations to remove ~60 lines.

---
## Fix Graphite divergence in incremental-dispatch after plumbing commit
### Context
`erk exec incremental-dispatch` uses `commit_files_to_branch` (git plumbing) to commit plan files to a branch without checkout. This advances the branch ref but bypasses Graphite, causing Graphite to report divergence. The fix already exists in `plan_save.py` (line 259-260) but was never applied to `incremental_dispatch.py`.
Secondary issue: when the branch is checked out in the current worktree, the index sync uses `git checkout HEAD -- <files>` which is fragile. `plan_save.py` uses the better pattern of writing files to disk + `stage_files(force=True)`.
### Changes
**File: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py`**
**1. Add Graphite retrack after plumbing commit (after line 132)**
```python # Update Graphite tracking after plumbing commit advanced the branch erk_ctx.branch_manager.retrack_branch(repo_root, branch_name) ```
**2. Replace index sync block (lines 134-144)**
Replace the `git checkout HEAD -- <files>` approach with `plan_save.py`'s pattern:
```python if checked_out_path is not None: for rel_path, content in files.items(): abs_path = checked_out_path / rel_path abs_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) abs_path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8") git.commit.stage_files(checked_out_path, list(files.keys()), force=True) ```
### Verification
1. Run incremental-dispatch tests: `pytest tests/ -k incremental_dispatch` 2. Manual verification with `gt log` after dispatch

session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="4f79cb1a-3196-4971-a4f8-4ffff314fc36" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="4f79cb1a-3196-4971-a4f8-4ffff314fc36" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "4f79cb1a-3196-4971-a4f8-4ffff314fc36" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "4f79cb1a-3196-4971-a4f8-4ffff314fc36" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "4f79cb1a-3196-4971-a4f8-4ffff314fc36" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "4f79cb1a-3196-4971-a4f8-4ffff314fc36" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
